asset_alt: Archived VLA safety standalone diagram with policy, attack, constraint, and safe rollout gates
asset_caption: Archive diagram: VLA safety kept as task-specific guardrails rather than a standalone project.
title: Archive: BiSafeVLA / VLA Safety Standalone Track
description: Archive reason: VLA safety alignment and attack benchmarking are now covered by SafeVLA and AttackVLA-style work; keep safety as constraints inside active robot projects.
summary: The standalone VLA safety card is archived because current papers already provide the safety-alignment and attack-benchmark framing. For this dashboard, safety should be task-specific guardrails and verification criteria, not a separate survey project yet.

details.text: SafeVLA frames constrained-learning safety alignment for VLA models and demonstrates sim-to-real safety transfer; AttackVLA supplies a unified adversarial/backdoor benchmark with real/sim robot settings. Together they cover the generic standalone VLA-safety topic well enough for this dashboard.

details: Moved-out live work: use these as safety references for Superhuman Robot Execution, Real-Robot Infra, Human CAPTCHA, and any VLA deployment gate.
Archive decision: no separate Survey card until we have a local VLA policy and need a safety benchmark implementation.
risks_decisions: Reason type: externally covered and currently lacks a local implementation target.
Reopen condition: a deployed or trained VLA in this portfolio needs a local safety-alignment evaluation suite.
